title: jacobaea × calvescens
intro: Jacobaea × calvescens, also known as Woolly Knapweed, is a hybrid plant species within the sunflower family, characterized by its large, woolly flower heads. Traditionally, Woolly Knapweed has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Woolly Knapweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and therapeutic benefits.
